body.title: Librarian — research peer-agent + bidirectional growth loop with Igor
body.summary: Librarian is a peer agent that shares Igor's constitutional layer but runs a different cognitive architecture, so where the two agree a belief is well-anchored and where they diverge one has a blind spot or better information. The growth-loop intent makes that divergence VISIBLE both ways: after each research/synthesis cycle Librarian emits a kind='librarian_observation' onto instance.proposals — the SAME observation bus Igor's dreaming uses — recording topic, outcome (answered/failed/escalated), confidence, tier and effective-source count, deduped by fingerprint with occurrence_count accumulating repeated failures. Igor's dreaming pass reads those observations, so a topic Librarian repeatedly fails is structurally identical to a recurring-failure pattern dreaming already handles. No new tables, no new memory types — instance.proposals is the bidirectional observation bus.

key_concepts.name: shared constitution, different architecture
key_concepts.what: agreement = anchored belief; divergence = a blind spot or better info in one of them — the signal worth surfacing

key_concepts.name: instance.proposals as observation bus
key_concepts.what: Librarian writes librarian_observation entries to the same table dreaming uses; reused bidirectionally, no new schema

key_concepts.name: dedup + occurrence_count
key_concepts.what: fingerprint dedup; repeated failures on a topic increment occurrence_count, accumulating signal like dreaming's recurring patterns

key_concepts.name: dreaming consumes the loop
key_concepts.what: Igor's dreaming reads Librarian observations, closing a bidirectional growth loop (previously one-way: Akien -> both)

body.status_notes: Librarian is a live rack device with no prior intent-point. The growth-loop spec describes the observation emission (PalaceWriter.write / ResearchEngine on failure) and dreaming's _read_librarian_observations consumer; verify the emit/consume hooks are wired before treating the loop as proven. The 'instance.proposals' table is the Igor-side cognition store (Postgres, NO SQLITE), distinct from the dev-process filesystem store.
